root_cause_candidates.cause: Frontend reports a memory fault but no observable upstream error logs or downstream anomalies in the provided window. With no corroborating logs from upstream services and the deterministic TCB-RCA not identifying a separate root cause beyond the symptom service, the root cause remains unresolved or could be an internal frontend memory/state issue.
root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All sources provide limited or conflicting signals. Log Agent finds no error logs, offering no upstream/root-cause direction. Topology shows frontend depends on multiple upstream services, but does not pinpoint any specific one due to lack of observed faults in logs. TCB-RCA ranks frontend as the fault propagation node but reports no anomalies to back that claim. In the absence of concrete evidence pointing to a deeper upstream component, the most plausible single root-cause designation under current data is that the frontend itself is the observed symptom/source of the mem fault, or that the issue lies within frontend state/memory not reflected in logs within the sampled window.

metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends directly on seven services. Any of these could be the upstream fault propagating to the symptom. The blast radius currently includes only frontend, but the plausible propagation path is from any of the listed dependencies to frontend. Alternative paths could involve a root cause deeper in the dependency graph of any of these services, but with the given topology we can confirm direct upstream paths are structurally plausible. The topology indicates that the most critical services to monitor within the blast radius are the major commerce and data services (cartservice, productcatalogservice, checkoutservice, shippingservice, currencyservice) as failures or latency there would most impact frontend functionality.

result.synthesis_reasoning: There is no strong convergence across the three sources. The Log Agent reports no error logs to causalize upstream propagation, the Topology Agent identifies plausible upstream dependencies but cannot isolate a single upstream fault due to the lack of observed anomalies, and the TCB-RCA Engine does not produce a concrete upstream root cause beyond the symptom (frontend). In line with the rules, we avoid inventing a deeper upstream root cause when evidence is genuinely weak. The most defensible conclusion with current evidence is that the root cause remains ambiguous and could be an internal memory issue within the frontend or an unlogged condition, requiring targeted investigation (memory profiling, extended logs, or metrics) to move beyond a weak-evidence stance.
